Program Objectives

Oral and Dental Health

Program Objectives

Mission-Vision

Academic Staff

Courses

Double Major

Internship

Program Objectives

Oral and dental health technicians are the health technicians, who work at dental clinics as assistants of dentists, aiding them in all clinical works. Oral and dental health technicians prepare the working area for the dentist, admit the patient and
    help the dentist and the patient during the treatment. The purpose of the oral and dental health technician program is to teach students, who would be of choice in both public and private sectors and are qualified and modern with levels of international
    knowledge in dental health.

Vertical Transfer Opportunities

Graduates of this program can transfer externally to the following undergraduate departments:

Emergency Rescue and Disaster Management,

Nursing,

Health Management and Social Services.

Career Opportunities

Students, who graduate from this program, can work at dental laboratories, dental clinics, dental health departments of hospitals and companies that provide technical equipment in dental health as “Oral and Dental Health Technician.”
